title: celtis bungeana var. jessoensis
intro: Celtis bungeana var. jessoensis, also known as the Siberian hackberry or Japanese hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of tannins and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, Celtis bungeana var. jessoensis is often made into a tea or infused in hot water to treat a range of ailments, and it is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
